title: gh#69: R3.4 — Switch worker/twitter-delete (auto-destruct) to TokenVault
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/69.
Backlog section: Candidate 3 — TokenVault.

## Context

Fourth PR in Candidate 3. The auto-destruct path's Twitter delete service decrypts the same 4 OAuth 1.0a credentials as TwitterPublisher does today. Migration mirrors R3.3 but smaller (unseal-only).

## Scope

- Modify `packages/worker/src/twitter-delete.service.ts`:
  - Inject `vault: TokenVault` via the auto-destruct worker factory
  - Replace the four `decrypt(...)` calls (line 66+) with a single `vault.unsealForProfile(profile)` + narrow to the Twitter variant
  - Remove `process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY` read inside the function
  - The plaintext discipline (function-scope const, no caching, no logging) is preserved — the vault just provides the unseal step
- Update `packages/worker/src/auto-destruct-worker.ts` (or wherever twitter-delete is wired) to construct/pass the vault
- Update twitter-delete tests to inject a fake vault

## Out of scope

- Publishers (R3.5)

## Acceptance

- `pnpm --filter @sms/worker build` and test pass
- `rg "decrypt\(" packages/worker/src/twitter-delete.service.ts` returns no matches
- `rg "process\.env\.ENCRYPTION_KEY" packages/worker/src/twitter-delete.service.ts` returns no matches

## References

- Depends on: #66

history.reasoning: Original evidence paths still exist. packages/worker/src/twitter-delete.service.ts still imports decrypt/validateEncryptionKey, reads process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY at line 39, and performs four decrypt(...) calls at lines 66, 72, 78, and 84. packages/worker/src/auto-destruct-worker.ts still calls deleteTweet({ profile, platformPostId, correlationId }) without constructing or passing a TokenVault. The current auto-destruct tests still mock @sms/shared/encryption and set process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY rather than injecting a fake vault. Acceptance checks for no decrypt(...) and no process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY in twitter-delete.service.ts both fail. Type checking with pnpm --filter @sms/worker exec tsc --noEmit passed; full build/test could not run in this read-only sandbox because tsc/vitest attempted to write dist/.vite-temp files and failed with EPERM.

The original evidence paths still exist, but the reported direct-decryption behavior has been replaced. packages/worker/src/twitter-delete.service.ts now accepts a TokenVault, calls vault.unsealForProfile(profile), narrows to twitter credentials, and constructs TwitterApi from those unsealed credentials; rg found no decrypt( or process.env.ENCRYPTION_KEY matches in that file. packages/worker/src/auto-destruct-worker.ts now requires deps.vault and passes vault: deps.vault into deleteTweet. The auto-destruct tests now inject a fake TokenVault and assert unsealForProfile is called. I attempted the requested verification commands: the focused vitest run could not start in this read-only sandbox because Vite tried to write packages/worker/node_modules/.vite-temp and got EPERM; pnpm --filter @sms/worker build also hit EPERM writing dist plus an unrelated TypeScript error in packages/worker/src/post-lifecycle.impl.ts(79,62); tsc --noEmit reported the same unrelated post-lifecycle error. Those failures do not indicate this TokenVault migration remains open.
